messages.content: Does Webb still offer AP courses?
No. Webb formally discontinued AP courses in fall 2025 and replaced them with Advanced Studies courses. The catalog also says AP exams are no longer administered on campus. [source: course catalog]
How many classes do Webb students have to take each semester?
The 2026-27 course catalog says students are required to take five courses each semester. Many students choose a sixth, but students are not permitted to take seven. [source: course catalog]
Do you have to play a sport at Webb?

Students participate in the afternoon program every season, and Webb requires at least one interscholastic sport season each year. Some alternatives can count in specific cases, but it is not an opt-out system. [source: course catalog]
Is community dinner actually required?

Yes for boarding students, and yes for day students if they are on campus that evening. The handbook says community dinners are held on Sunday evenings and Wednesday evenings at 6:00 p.m. [source: handbook]
Can students order DoorDash on a Tuesday night?
Not during the academic week. The handbook says students may not order or receive food deliveries during the academic week. [source: handbook]
Who should I contact about transcripts or records?
The registrar is the right first contact for transcripts and academic records. In the Webb materials, that office is listed under Briar Rose Tirpak. [source: handbook] [source: directory]
What is the difference between Honors and Advanced Studies at Webb?

According to the catalog, Honors courses add depth and advanced content, while Advanced Studies courses are the most advanced Webb-designed courses, built around deeper study, current research, and stronger interdisciplinary connections. [source: course catalog]
Can a Webb student take a class at the Claremont Colleges?

Possibly, but only after exhausting Webb's advanced offerings in that department and getting multiple approvals. The catalog describes it as an option for students who have completed all advanced-level course offerings in a given area. [source: course catalog]
How long do you have to add or drop a class?

For the 2026-27 catalog, the window is the first seven academic days of each semester. If you are asking about a different school year, check that year's handbook or catalog, because policy wording can change. [source: course catalog]
Can I switch sections just because I want a different teacher or a free block?

Not usually. Webb's course materials say students are scheduled by subject, not by teacher or time block, and requests for a specific teacher or free block will not be granted. [source: course catalog]
How many years of language does Webb want for selective colleges?

Webb's catalog lists three years of the same language as the school requirement and four years as the recommendation for highly selective colleges. UC requirements are lower than that. [source: course catalog]
What do you need before taking HMC/Webb Advanced Courses in Computer Science?
The catalog lists a concurrent level-appropriate math course as the prerequisite. It is also marked as requiring department approval. [source: course catalog]
How much fine arts do you need to graduate from Webb?
Two years of fine arts are listed in the graduation requirements. The catalog also notes that UC admission requires at least one year of fine arts. [source: course catalog]
Can my family hire any tutor to come work with me on campus?

Not just any tutor. Webb says outside tutors working on campus must be part of an approved and vetted tutor pool, and tutoring is expected to fit within school rules and approved spaces. [source: handbook] [source: course catalog]
What happens if I miss a lot of one class?

The handbook says repeated absences can trigger a meeting and added support, and a hearing for loss of credit may be held once a student records five absences in the same course in a semester. [source: handbook]
Is Webb an AP testing center?

No. The handbook says Webb does not offer the AP curriculum and is not a testing center for AP exams. The course catalog also says AP exams are no longer administered on campus. [source: handbook] [source: course catalog]
What are Chapel Talks at Webb?

Chapel Talks are morning talks given in Vivian Webb Chapel, and seniors are invited to craft one to share what they have learned during their time at Webb. They are described as expressions of growth, gratitude, and reflection. [source: chapel page]
What kinds of afternoon activities are there besides sports?

Quite a few. Webb lists options such as robotics, debate, technical theater, community service, museum lab, science fair research, dance, yearbook, literary magazine, and outdoor activities, depending on the season. [source: course catalog] [source: after-school page]
Does Webb let students use the name and pronouns they identify with?

Yes. The handbook says students have the right to be addressed by the name and pronoun that correspond to their gender identity, and intentional refusal to respect that is not tolerated. [source: handbook]
Are there any gender-neutral bathrooms on campus?
Yes. The handbook lists gender-neutral bathrooms in several campus locations and says they are available to all students. [source: handbook]
When are evening study hours for boarders?
The handbook says evening study hours run from 7 to 10 p.m. on Sundays through Thursdays, with supervised academic lab time and then individual quiet study. [source: handbook]
What does Webb say about college placement?

Webb presents itself as a long-standing college preparatory school and says 99% of graduates gain admission to selective four-year colleges and universities, while 1% choose an alternative such as a gap year. [source: college guidance profile]
Does Webb have a lot of clubs?

Yes. The clubs list is broad and includes things like AI Club, Biology Club, Coding Club, Finance Club, Model UN, Mental Health Awareness Club, Robotics-related groups, and many more. [source: clubs page]
Can juniors or seniors get team sport credit without being on a varsity roster?
Sometimes. The course catalog says juniors or seniors can earn team sport credit as a manager for certain programs, and sports broadcasting can also count in some cases. [source: course catalog]
What kinds of food options does the dining hall have?

Webb describes daily breakfast, lunch, and dinner with vegetarian options at every meal. Lunch options include things like a noodle bar, salad bar, soups, sandwich and panini stations, and Al's Patio. [source: dining page]
Should I take Honors Precalculus or regular Precalculus? I got an A- in my current math class but I also play a sport every season.

An A- suggests Honors Precalculus may be realistic, but the decision should depend on more than the letter grade. I would weigh three things: how hard you had to work for that A-, how strong your algebra and functions foundation is, and how much time your sport and other classes take. If your current math class feels solid rather than barely manageable, Honors could make sense. If you are already stretched thin, regular Precalculus may be the better choice because a strong grade in the right level is better than being overloaded.
What does workload 1 versus 1.25 mean in the catalog?

At Webb, those numbers are a rough signal of expected intensity. A course marked 1.25 is generally meant to be heavier than a course marked 1, especially in homework pace, reading, problem sets, or project demands. It is not the only thing to use when choosing classes, but it is a useful warning sign when you are building a balanced schedule.
I'm interested in both CS and biology. What would a strong 11th grade plan look like?

A solid plan would usually keep one clear focus and one supporting interest. For example: a strong math course, HMC/Webb CS if you are ready for it, one serious science such as an advanced biology-related option when available, a humanities course that keeps your transcript balanced, and your world language or arts progression if you still need it. The main mistake to avoid is stacking every hardest STEM course at once. A strong schedule should still be sustainable.
Can you explain weighted versus unweighted GPA in plain English?

Unweighted GPA treats all courses the same. Weighted GPA gives extra value to harder classes. At Webb, the handbook says Honors courses get a 0.5 boost and Advanced Studies courses get a 1.0 boost in the weighted GPA. So two students with the same letter grades could have different weighted GPAs if one took more advanced classes. [source: handbook]
What is a prerequisite?
A prerequisite is a class or skill you are expected to complete before taking a more advanced course. It is there to make sure you have the background needed to succeed.
Which is better for someone creative: Media Arts or Filmmaking Studio I?

They are creative in different ways. Media Arts is broader and more digital-design oriented, with tools for illustration, animation, graphic design, game design, and 3D work. Filmmaking Studio I is more focused on video storytelling, camera work, lighting, sound, editing, and short film production. If you like visual design across multiple formats, start with Media Arts. If you specifically want to make films, start with Filmmaking Studio I. [source: fine arts page]
What's the difference between Theater and Technical Theater in the afternoon program?

Theater is centered on acting and performance. Technical Theater is about the non-performance side of production, such as lighting, sound, set construction, design, safety, and backstage operations. If you like being on stage, Theater is the clearer fit. If you like building shows rather than performing in them, Technical Theater is probably better. [source: course catalog]
What does department approval actually mean?

It means enrollment is not automatic even if you meet the listed prerequisite. The department can still look at your grades, readiness, pace, maturity in the subject, and overall schedule before deciding whether the course is a good fit.
How do I build a balanced schedule if I also care a lot about leadership and sports?

Start by treating time as a real limit, not an afterthought. Pick the classes that matter most for your long-term goals, then ask whether your non-academic commitments leave room to do those classes well. In practice, that often means taking one or two major academic stretches, not four or five at once. Webb's own guidance emphasizes that challenging and appropriate is the goal, not maximum difficulty at every slot. [source: course catalog]
For senior year, is Statistics or Calculus the smarter choice?

It depends on what you want after high school. Calculus is usually the stronger signal for engineering, physics, and some selective STEM pathways. Statistics can be a smart choice for social science, business, psychology, biology, data-oriented fields, or for students who want rigorous math without the specific style of calculus. The better course is the one that matches your goals and that you can do well in.
What should I take before AdvSt Chemistry?

The catalog lists Integrated Math II with a B+ or better and Precalculus as a concurrent course. So the basic idea is that Webb expects a solid math foundation before and during that class. [source: course catalog]
Can you explain the Webb difference between Honors and Advanced Studies in one simple comparison?

A simple way to think about it is this: Honors is a harder version of strong high school work, while Advanced Studies is closer to Webb's most intensive college-style work. Both are rigorous, but Advanced Studies usually asks for more independence and depth.
Can you help me write a short email to my advisor asking about an honors appeal?

Sure. You could send this:

Hi [Advisor Name],
I wanted to ask about the process for appealing into [Course Name]. I understand the course expectations are high, and I would appreciate your guidance on whether an appeal makes sense for me and what I should prepare. Thank you for your time.

Best,
[Your Name]
I drown in humanities reading. What's a better way to handle it?

Try reading in layers instead of treating every page the same. First, identify the argument or main question. Second, annotate only the lines that move that argument, reveal tone, or show a pattern. Third, pause every few pages and say the point in your own words. That makes class discussion and writing much easier, and it keeps you from spending energy on details you will not use.
How should I decide between Chinese 4 and AdvSt Chinese?

Ask yourself whether you want continuation or stretch. Chinese 4 is the safer choice if you want solid growth and room for other priorities. AdvSt Chinese makes more sense if your foundation is already strong, you genuinely enjoy the subject, and you have the time for a faster and deeper course. The right answer is not just which one looks harder. It is which one you can use well.
What does an effort mark of 3 mean?

At Webb, an effort mark of 3 means needs improvement. The handbook describes it as below-average application to the course and says it can involve things like late work, weak class participation, poor use of help opportunities, or inconsistent habits. [source: handbook]
Explain excused versus unexcused absences like I'm new here.

An excused absence is one the school recognizes through the proper process, such as illness, a school-sponsored activity, a college pass, or a documented family emergency. An unexcused absence is one without an accepted formal excuse. The difference matters because missed graded work during an unexcused absence can receive a failing grade and the absence can lead to discipline. [source: handbook]
I like humanities but I also want engineering. Does that mean my schedule has to be all STEM?

No. For engineering, you do need strong math and science preparation, but a schedule that shows serious humanities work can still be a strength. Good engineering applicants are not just calculators. They also read closely, write clearly, and think across disciplines. The real priority is making sure your math progression stays strong while the rest of your schedule remains coherent.
Is office hours or peer tutoring better if I'm stuck in one class?

Usually start with office hours because your teacher knows the course goals, grading standards, and the exact skill gap. Peer tutoring is a good supplement when you need more repetition or a student-level explanation. Outside tutoring should be the last step, not the first.
Why doesn't dropping AP automatically hurt students at Webb?

Because course rigor is not defined by the AP label alone. Webb says it replaced AP with Advanced Studies courses and continued to frame the curriculum as rigorous and college preparatory. Colleges read in context. What matters most is the level of the work, the strength of your performance, and how your school explains its program.
Is Mr. Hamilton definitely teaching HMC/Webb CS next year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Faculty roles can change, so the safest move is to check the current department listing or ask the school directly.
Did I get selected for a leadership role yet?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. If you have an email, announcement, or message you want me to interpret, paste it here and I can help read it carefully.
What's the exact date of prom next year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. School event dates can change year to year, so I would check the current school calendar or student announcements.
Does Northwestern love Webb applicants?

I can't confirm that from the information I have, and I would be careful with that kind of claim anyway. Webb clearly sends students to highly selective colleges, but no college has a guaranteed preference you should rely on.
Will my honors appeal definitely get approved if I got an A last semester?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. A strong grade helps, but appeals can also depend on department judgment, course fit, and your overall load.
What's for lunch next Tuesday?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Dining menus change, so you would need the current meal schedule or dining announcement to know for sure.
Is there a school shuttle leaving at 6:15 tonight?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Transportation details are usually time-sensitive, so I would check the current student communication channel or the appropriate office.
Can I move dorms next semester if I ask nicely?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Housing decisions depend on availability and school judgment, so this is something only residential leadership can decide.
Was that student suspended?
I can't confirm that from the information I have, and student discipline is also private. If your concern is safety or a policy issue, the better move is to bring it to an adult at school.
What time does AI Club meet this week?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Club times are usually handled through current student communications, so I would check the latest schedule rather than rely on an old list.
If I take an online summer class somewhere else, will Webb automatically count it?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. External coursework usually needs prior approval, so I would not assume it will count unless the school says yes in advance.
Is there definitely a winter tennis clinic next year?

I can't confirm that from the information I have. Webb has listed winter tennis clinic in recent materials, but seasonal offerings can change by year. Check the current afternoon program when it comes out.
Did Webb's admit rate go up this year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. I do not want to guess on admission numbers without a current official source.
Can I take two off-campus college classes while enrolled at Webb?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Off-campus coursework depends on approvals, schedule conflicts, and school policy, so you would need a direct answer from Webb.
Is Dorm Prefect always better than Peer Advisor for college apps?

I can't confirm that from the information I have because colleges do not rank roles by a fixed formula. They care more about what you actually did, how sustained the work was, and how well it fits the rest of your profile.
Can you tell me about the policy?
Which policy do you mean: attendance, tutoring, course changes, dress code, off-campus passes, or something else?
I need help picking classes.
What grade are you planning for, and are you trying to optimize for rigor, GPA, workload balance, or a specific interest like STEM, humanities, arts, or athletics?
Is this enough?
Enough for what goal? If you mean college rigor, graduation requirements, language years, leadership, or volunteer hours, the answer could be very different.
Can I get an exception?
An exception to what exactly: a prerequisite, a course level, an absence policy, a pass rule, or something else?
What should I email them?
Who are you emailing, and what outcome do you want from the message?
Should I drop it?
Which class is it, how far into the term are you, and is the problem the grade, the workload, or the fit?
I want to do research at Webb.
Do you mean academic-year research, summer research, museum research, science fair work, or independent study? Also, what field are you interested in?
How hard is it?
Which course or activity are you asking about?
Can you compare these?
Yes, but I need the two options first.
What do I do about the form?
Which form is it, and what are you trying to get approved?
Can you summarize the handbook part for me?
Yes. Which section do you want summarized: academics, absences, tutoring, dining, dorm life, community rules, or discipline?
I need a better schedule.
Send me your grade, your current course list, and your biggest outside commitments, and I can help you rebuild it.
What are my options after school?
Do you want athletics, arts, academic activities, or a mix of those?
Is it worth it?
What are you deciding between, and what matters most to you: enjoyment, college value, skill-building, time, or stress?
Can you rewrite this message?
Paste the message and tell me the tone you want, like polite, direct, warm, formal, or brief.
hey
Hey. What do you need help with?
Can you summarize this for me in two sentences? I was overwhelmed reading it.
Yes. Paste the text and I will condense it without losing the main point.
Can you just do my homework answer for me even though I didn't read the chapter?
I can help, but I should not fake work you did not do. Send the question or the chapter section, and I can help you understand it fast enough to write an honest answer.
I have too much to do tonight and I'm kind of panicking.

Start smaller than your brain wants to. Make one list, circle the two tasks with real deadlines, do the easier of those two first for 15 minutes, then reassess. Panic usually shrinks once you are moving.
Make this email less rude: I need you to send the grade update now because this is taking way too long.

Here is a more professional version:

Hi [Name],
I wanted to follow up and ask whether there is an update on the grade. I would appreciate it if you could let me know when you have a moment. Thank you.

Best,
[Your Name]
What does matriculate mean?
It means to officially enroll in a school or college. In school profile language, matriculation usually refers to where students actually ended up attending.
thanks
You're welcome.
Can you draft a quick apology email for missing a meeting?

Sure:

Hi [Name],
I am sorry for missing the meeting today. That was my mistake. I apologize for the inconvenience, and I would be grateful for the chance to reschedule or catch up on what I missed.

Best,
[Your Name]
I got a B+ and now I feel like I ruined everything.

A B+ is not everything ruined. It is one data point. The real question is whether it reflects a temporary rough patch, a course that was mis-leveled, or a study method that needs adjustment. That is a fixable conversation.
Write a fake excuse note saying I was sick.
I can't help write a false excuse. If you need, I can help you draft an honest note explaining what happened and asking how to make up the missed work.
Give me a study plan for tonight. I have a math quiz and a history reading.

Do math first while your concentration is fresher. Spend 35 to 45 minutes on the quiz material, then take a short break, then do the history reading with a clear goal: identify the thesis, three supporting points, and one question for class. Finish by spending 10 minutes checking tomorrow's deadlines so nothing sneaks up on you.
Can you turn these messy notes into clean bullet points? We talked about adding a course, maybe switching levels, and meeting again next week.

Yes:
- Discussed the possibility of adding a course.
- Considered whether a level change might be a better fit.
- Agreed to meet again next week after reviewing the schedule.
What's the difference between Northwestern and Northeastern?

They are completely different universities. Northwestern is a private research university near Chicago. Northeastern is a private research university in Boston known especially for co-op and career-integrated study. A lot of confusion comes from the names sounding similar, but they are not related.
Write me a brutal message roasting my teacher.
I can help you write a firm complaint, but not a cruel one. If you want, I can turn your frustration into a message that is direct, credible, and more likely to be taken seriously.
Make this sentence clearer: I maybe sort of want to appeal but also not if it's too much because my schedule is already bad.
Here is a cleaner version: I am considering an appeal, but I am unsure whether it is the right choice because my current schedule already feels heavy.
I'm choosing 11th grade classes. Right now I'm thinking AdvSt Calculus I, AdvSt Chemistry, HMC/Webb CS, an advanced humanities elective, and varsity tennis. Is that smart?

That is a very ambitious schedule. The biggest issue is not whether each piece is impressive on its own, but whether all of them together leave room for strong performance and sleep. For most students, that combination would be high risk.
What would you cut first?

I would first question whether you need both the heaviest science option and HMC/Webb CS at the same time. If CS is a priority, I would usually preserve the math and CS path, then decide whether the science or humanities intensity should come down a level.
I saw one place say add drop is seven days and another say ten. Which is right?

Both may be right for different years. The 2026-27 course catalog says seven academic days, while the 2025-26 handbook says ten academic days. If you are making a real decision, use the policy for the specific school year you are in. [source: course catalog] [source: handbook]
Between Dorm Prefect and Peer Advisor, which one is better?

Neither is automatically better in every case. Dorm Prefect often signals day-to-day residential responsibility and trust. Peer Advisor often signals mentorship and support. Colleges usually care less about the title than about the actual work and whether it clearly fits your broader story.
So which one should I pick if I already do a lot of mentoring?

If mentoring is already a real theme in your activities, Peer Advisor may create a cleaner narrative. If you want to show a different kind of leadership, Dorm Prefect could broaden your profile. The better choice is usually the one you will do more genuinely and more well.
Is Hon Musem Rsrch the class after paleontology? sorry typo
I think you mean Honors Museum Research. In the catalog, Honors Museum Research lists Honors Paleontology as a prerequisite, along with a concurrent second science. [source: course catalog]
Answer with only confirmed information from official Webb materials. If something is not confirmed, say so clearly.
Can students self-study for AP exams and miss Webb obligations for them?

The course catalog says students are strongly discouraged from registering for AP exams at external testing sites, and students who choose to do so will not be excused from Webb academic or extracurricular obligations. [source: course catalog]
Be careful with uncertainty. Do not guess schedules, dates, or decisions.
What room will Science Fair Research Lab be in next winter?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The materials describe the program, but not a fixed room assignment for a future season.
Can you answer my question and also draft the email? I want to ask whether I can level down without it looking bad.
Yes. In general, a level change is not automatically bad. It depends on why it happened, when it happened, and whether the change helped you get into a more appropriate level.
Okay draft it to my advisor.

Hi [Advisor Name],
I wanted to ask whether a level change might be appropriate for me in [Course Name]. I want to make sure I am in a class level where I can learn well and manage my overall schedule responsibly. Could we talk about whether that would make sense and what the process would be?

Best,
[Your Name]
When giving scheduling advice, prioritize balance over maximum rigor.
I want the hardest possible schedule, varsity athletics, leadership, and also eight hours of sleep. How do I do that?

You probably cannot maximize all of those at once. Something has to give. The realistic goal is a schedule that is clearly rigorous but still sustainable. That usually means picking the advanced classes that matter most for your interests instead of trying to turn every category to maximum at the same time.
What's the best loophole if I want to take seven classes anyway?

I can't help with finding loopholes around school rules. The catalog says students are not permitted to take seven courses. A better move is to figure out which class matters least right now or whether one of your goals can be pushed to a later year. [source: course catalog]
Use short source notes when facts come from Webb documents. Keep the tone plain and helpful.
Can you give me a short explanation of how Webb handles outside tutoring?

Webb allows outside tutoring, but it is structured. The school emphasizes teachers and office hours first, keeps an approved pool of vetted tutors for on-campus work, and says students should not miss Webb commitments for tutoring appointments. [source: handbook] [source: course catalog]
